hypothesis: The computational cost of HMC refinement in AMDEN scales prohibitively with system size and number of denoising steps; therefore, replacing the uniform HMC sampling schedule with an adaptive schedule that concentrates HMC iterations preferentially at intermediate diffusion times where the noise-energy landscape transitions from smooth to rugged (identifiable by the divergence point between forward and denoising trajectories, approximately t = 0.4 as observed in the paper) will achieve equivalent structural relaxation quality with significantly fewer total model evaluations, because barrier-crossing events critical for escaping metastable configurations are concentrated in this transitional regime rather than distributed uniformly across all diffusion timesteps.
